evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-auth/plan.md. Assume checklists/ exists with ux.md (all complete) and test.md (3 incomplete items). Produce the exact user-facing response for the checklist gate step and what happens next.
evals.expected_output: The run reports checklist table with failing checklist, asks whether to proceed, and halts pending user confirmation.
expectations: Includes a checklist status table with columns Checklist, Total, Completed, Incomplete, and Status
Includes the exact gate question: Some checklists are incomplete. Do you want to proceed with implementation anyway? (yes/no)
States that execution pauses or halts pending user confirmation

evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-export/plan.md. Assume plan.md exists but tasks.md is missing. Show the exact handling behavior after context loading begins.
evals.expected_output: The run fails fast with a clear message that tasks.md is required and recommends /create-tasks before proceeding.
expectations: Explicitly states that tasks.md is missing
Instructs the user to run /create-tasks (or regenerate planning artifacts)
States that execution is halted or blocked before phase execution

evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-notify/plan.md. Assume all checklists pass, tasks.md has Setup, Tests, Core, Integration, Polish phases with mixed [P] markers and overlapping file paths. Produce a concise execution handoff format for one phase plus the checkpoint criteria before moving to the next phase.
evals.expected_output: The run provides explicit phase handoff details, safe parallelization rules, TDD ordering, and checkpoint validation requirements before advancing.
expectations: Includes all five progress reporting sections: Checklist Gate, Implementation Context Loaded, Phase Execution, Code Review Findings, Completion Validation
Explains that [P] tasks can run in parallel only when touched file paths do not overlap
Defines a checkpoint decision format or equivalent explicit pass/fail criteria before advancing
References TDD-first ordering where test tasks run before their corresponding implementation tasks

evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-review-scope/plan.md. The implementation changed these uncommitted files: src/api/routes.ts, src/core/service.ts, docs/notes.md, and .gitignore. Produce the Code Review Findings section, including scope definition and completion gating.
evals.expected_output: Code review scope is derived from uncommitted changed files (staged/unstaged/untracked), excludes .gitignore, reviews all remaining files, and reports measurable coverage counts with missing-file gating.
expectations: Defines review scope using all uncommitted changed files rather than only phase-touched files
Explicitly excludes .gitignore from review scope
Includes a Review Scope Coverage block with Total Changed (Uncommitted) Files, Total Reviewed Files, Missing Files, and Missing File List
Includes Excluded Files and Completion Gate lines in the Review Scope Coverage template
States that Missing Files > 0 means code review is incomplete unless explicitly deferred/approved

evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-delete-edge/plan.md. Uncommitted git status includes: M src/a.ts, D src/legacy.ts, ?? docs/new.md, M .gitignore. Produce only the Code Review Findings section and ensure deleted and .gitignore handling are correctly represented.
evals.expected_output: The response computes scope from uncommitted changes, excludes deleted files and .gitignore, includes deterministic coverage metrics, and reports the completion gate line exactly.
expectations: Excludes deleted files from review scope while still accounting for them in Excluded Files
Excludes .gitignore from review scope and reports it under Excluded Files
Uses Review Scope Coverage template fields including Completion Gate
Shows Missing Files and Missing File List consistent with reviewed non-excluded files

evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-scope-drift/plan.md. Assume staged files are src/staged_api.ts and docs/staged_notes.md, unstaged files are src/unstaged_service.ts, and untracked file is scripts/new_check.sh. A reviewer subagent proposes reviewing only src/staged_api.ts. Produce Code Review Findings that prevents scope drift.
evals.expected_output: The response enforces a single materialized review_scope_files list across all review subagents, rejects narrower per-agent recomputation, and marks completion as incomplete when files are omitted.
expectations: Materializes review_scope_files from staged + unstaged + untracked files
States subagents must not recompute or narrow scope independently
Reports missing files when only a subset is reviewed
Applies Completion Gate with INCOMPLETE status when Missing Files > 0

evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-controller-conflict/plan.md. The controller generated review_scope_files as: src/api.ts, src/service.ts, docs/notes.md. Reviewer-A reports reviewing src/api.ts and src/service.ts. Reviewer-B reports reviewing src/api.ts and scripts/helper.sh. Produce Code Review Findings that resolves this conflict safely.
evals.expected_output: The response treats reviewer list mismatch as a scope conflict, reconciles against controller review_scope_files, reports missing/extra files, and keeps completion status INCOMPLETE until reconciled or explicitly deferred.
expectations: References controller review_scope_files as the authoritative source
Identifies mismatch between reviewer-reported lists and authoritative scope
Reports missing and extra files based on reconciliation
Marks completion as INCOMPLETE until mismatch is resolved or explicitly deferred/approved

evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-dashboard/plan.md. Assume all checklists pass. tasks.md has 15 tasks across Setup, Tests, Core, Integration, and Polish phases. Tasks T001-T006 are already marked [X] (complete) — these cover all of Setup and the first two tasks of Tests phase. Show how you handle resuming from this partially completed state.
evals.expected_output: The run detects completed tasks, skips them, reports a resumption summary with completed vs remaining counts, and begins execution from the first incomplete task.
expectations: Identifies that tasks T001-T006 are already marked complete
Reports a count or summary of completed vs remaining tasks
Does not re-execute already completed tasks
Begins execution from the first incomplete task in the current phase

evals.prompt: You are executing /implement-plan for plan_file=.agents/scratchpad/feature-api/plan.md. Assume all checklists pass. In the Core phase, tasks.md lists T007 (write API endpoint tests in tests/api_test.py) and T008 (implement API endpoint in src/api/routes.py). Both are sequential tasks in the Core phase. Show the execution plan for this phase, specifically explaining the ordering of these two tasks.
evals.expected_output: The run executes the test task T007 before the implementation task T008, following the TDD-first approach where tests are written before the code they verify.
expectations: Executes or plans to execute test task T007 before implementation task T008
References TDD-first or test-first approach as the reason for this ordering
Does not execute T008 before its corresponding test task T007 completes
